chunks.title: Components & Setup
chunks.content:
## Per Player

| Item | Amount |
|------|--------|
| Wonder board | 1 (random or drafted) |
| Starting coins | 3 |
| Cards dealt per age | 7 |
| Starting resource | 1 (shown top-left of Wonder board — always free) |

## Deck Preparation

Each Age deck contains cards labeled with a minimum player count. Before dealing, remove any card whose player-count symbol **exceeds** the number of players. Shuffle the remaining cards and deal 7 to each player.

## Card Pass Direction

| Age | Pass direction |
|-----|---------------|
| I   | Left (clockwise) |
| II  | Right (counter-clockwise) |
| III | Left (clockwise) |

chunks.title: Turn Structure
chunks.content:
All players act **simultaneously** each round.

## Each Round

| Step | Action |
|------|--------|
| 1 | Pick 1 card from your hand, place face-down |
| 2 | All players reveal simultaneously |
| 3 | Take your chosen action (see below) |
| 4 | Pass remaining hand in the age's direction |

## Actions (pick exactly one)

| Action | How |
|--------|-----|
| **Build the card** | Pay its cost; add face-up to your city |
| **Build a Wonder stage** | Slide card face-down under your Wonder board; pay the stage cost |
| **Discard for coins** | Place card on the discard pile; take **3 coins** from the bank |

## End of Age

After 6 rounds (1 card remains in each hand — discard it), resolve **military conflicts** with both neighbors before dealing the next Age.

chunks.title: How to Read a Card
chunks.content:
[DIAGRAM]
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ ①Cost          ②Chain-from │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│         ③Card Name          │
│      ④Category Color Band   │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│                             │
│           ⑤Artwork          │
│                             │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│  ⑥Effect symbols      ⑦VP │
├─────────────────────────────┤
│          ⑧Chain-to          │
└─────────────────────────────┘

[LEGEND]
① Cost: Coins (yellow) and/or resource icons required to build; absent = free
② Chain-from: Icon of an Age I/II card — if you already own it, build this card for free
③ Card Name: The building's name
④ Category Color Band: Brown=Raw Material · Grey=Manufactured Good · Blue=Civic · Yellow=Commercial · Green=Scientific · Red=Military · Purple=Guild
⑤ Artwork: Illustration — no game effect
⑥ Effect: What the card provides — resources, coins, shields, science symbol, or special power
⑦ VP: Victory points scored at end of game (present only on cards that award VP)
⑧ Chain-to: Symbol printed on this card — a future card referencing this symbol in its ②-slot can be built for free

chunks.title: Card Categories
chunks.content:
## Category Quick Reference

| Color | Category | What it does |
|-------|----------|--------------|
| Brown | Raw Materials | Produces Wood, Stone, Ore, or Clay |
| Grey | Manufactured Goods | Produces Glass, Papyrus, or Loom |
| Blue | Civic Buildings | Straight VP at end of game |
| Yellow | Commercial | Coins, trading discounts, or neighbor-dependent VP |
| Green | Scientific | Provides Gear ⚙, Compass 📐, or Tablet 📜 symbols |
| Red | Military | Shields — compared against both neighbors at each Age end |
| Purple | Guilds (Age III only) | Special scoring based on your city or neighbors' cards |

## Trading Rule

By default, buying a resource your neighbors produce costs **2 coins** (paid to that neighbor). Yellow commercial cards can reduce this to **1 coin** for specific resource types.

chunks.title: End-Game Scoring
chunks.content:
## Victory Point Sources

| Category | How scored |
|----------|------------|
| **Military** | Sum of conflict tokens: +1 / +3 / +5 per Age I/II/III victory; −1 per defeat |
| **Treasury** | 1 VP per 3 coins remaining (round down) |
| **Wonders** | VP printed on each completed stage |
| **Civic (Blue)** | Face-value VP printed on each card |
| **Commercial (Yellow)** | VP printed on the card (varies — some score neighbors' cards) |
| **Guilds (Purple)** | VP printed on the card (scored at game end, typically neighbor-dependent) |
| **Scientific (Green)** | See formula below |

## Science Scoring

Count your Gear ⚙, Compass 📐, and Tablet 📜 symbols separately.

| Source | Formula |
|--------|---------|
| Identical symbols | N² VP per group (e.g. 4 Gears = 16 VP) |
| Complete sets | +7 VP per set of all 3 different symbols |

**Example:** 3 Gears + 2 Compasses + 1 Tablet = 9 + 4 + 1 + 7 = **21 VP**

## Tiebreaker

Highest coin total wins.

chunks.title: First-Time Tips
chunks.content:
- **Don't ignore military entirely.** Even 1–2 shields can flip losses to draws against a weak neighbor, saving several points across three ages.
- **Science compounds fast.** Three different symbols already scores 7 bonus VP; doubling up on one symbol (N²) rewards specialization equally. Prioritize green if neighbors aren't racing it.
- **Chaining is free money.** Always check the ②-slot before paying full cost — a matching card from a previous age builds this one for free.
- **Discard for coins when stuck.** Three coins is better than playing a card that helps nobody. Coins also contribute VP at game end (1 VP per 3 coins).
- **Your Wonder board is your identity.** Build stages whenever their effect is strong — many wonders provide resources, which removes dependency on trading all game.
- **Watch your right neighbor in Age II.** The hand passes right, so you're drafting from the same pool your right neighbor just saw. Deny what they need if it costs you little.
- **Blue cards are safe but slow.** Civic VP is reliable; don't over-invest at the expense of resources or military you need early.
